title: Should you use AI for a task? Here’s a simple way to decide
excerpt: Sometimes, what matters isn’t your output but what you put into the process. Think of it like work v the gym
content_html:
I teach public policy at the Harvard Kennedy School and the Munk School at the University of Toronto. And it will come as no surprise to you that my students regularly use AI to complete their writing assignments. Doing so is a waste of their tuition money. But if their entire career is going to include AI writing assistants, why shouldn’t they embrace their future?

The best way I’ve found to explain the dilemma comes from the AI researcher Daniel Meissler: it’s the difference between work and the gym.

At work, if your job is to move a bunch of heavy things from one side of the room to another, you should use whatever assistive tech you have on hand: a wagon, a forklift ... even an AI-powered robot. But at the gym, it makes no sense for that robot to lift weights for you. The point of weightlifting isn’t to move heavy things across the room; it’s to actually lift those heavy things.

The same analysis holds for any task an AI can do for you. If it’s work – if the task has to be done and no one cares how – then it’s fine to use AI assistance. But if the task is more like the gym, and how the task is done is at least as important, then it probably doesn’t make sense to use AI.

This, of course, assumes that the AI is actually up for the task and that it’s trustworthy: that it can do the job well, that its mistakes are minimal and correctable, that it’s been secured from cyber-attacks that would influence its results. Those are all important, and shouldn’t be minimized. There’s no point giving an AI something that it can’t do reliably. But once you’re confident that the AI can perform the task, the work v gym distinction helps you decide if it should.

The writing assignments I give my students are gym tasks, not work tasks. I ask them to write policy memos not because the world needs more policy memos. I assign them because the very act of writing, which includes thinking and outlining and drafting and editing, making and criticizing and revising arguments, will help develop the critical thinking skills they will need in their future careers. And without this constant mental exercise, those skills will atrophy. Employers are already noticing.

Reading the assignments they turn in, I can see those skills either flourishing or atrophying in my students. At least today, I can pretty easily tell the difference between an AI-written memo and a student-written one – especially if the student just turns in what the chatbot produces. It’s a catchy, plausible, grammatically perfect essay that’s not particularly well-crafted or logically coherent – and with all the tells of mid-2026 AI-generated writing.

But it’s precisely because I have spent years developing my own writing skills that I’m able to identify prose that sounds great but doesn’t actually make sense. My students don’t have that skill; they mistakenly view a confident, well-written essay as evidence of the quality of their ideas. They see the AI as cleaning those ideas up, getting them through that uncomfortable stretch of having to turn those ideas into prose. What the students miss is that their initial discomfort is a normal and healthy stage of writing, and not something to quickly get beyond. The very act of struggling with how to express what they think is an important part of the process. It’s how they test out their ideas, examine their hypotheses, and actually figure out what they think. Homework is not work; it’s the gym.

Work v gym also helps us understand the problem facing creatives of all kinds.

Most of the time when someone hires a writer, they just need the words. They need an instruction manual for a piece of equipment, a detailed sales presentation, a government-mandated disclosure document, or a legal brief. They need dry, predictable, accurate writing: a piece of work, exactly what AIs are good at today and what I don’t want in my student assignments. Only sometimes is writing an art form – a book, a poem, an uplifting political speech. That kind of writing is more like the gym: process matters just as much as product.

For most of human history, the only option for all of these tasks was human writers. We hired one regardless of whether we needed work writing or gym writing. And that paid a lot of writers’ salaries. I know fiction writers who supported that poorly paying career with lucrative technical writing work. Now, for the first time in human history, we can separate out when we need writing as work and when we want writing as gym. And if AI can do most of the work-type writing, society doesn’t need as many human writers.

It’s the same for visual artists. Sometimes we need an actual artist, but most of the time we just need an image: a corporate mascot, a “beware of the dog” sign, or a packaging label. Historically we gave those jobs to artists, and sometimes beautiful art resulted. But most of the time it was just work. And, as it turns out, the world needs less pure art than simple images.

Explaining the problem isn’t the same as providing the solution. I give my students the “work versus gym” speech every class, but they still use AI. I have sympathy: assignments are hard, everyone is overworked and overstressed, and – most importantly – students feel like they’ll look bad in comparison if their peers are all using AI. Even if they don’t want to use the technology, they feel like they have no choice.

There’s also an incentive problem. No one pays us to go to the gym; maintaining healthy habits requires discipline. For me, the payoffs to exercise – fewer aches and pains, less fatigue, better mood/stress management – might make me a better writer and teacher, but they’re subtle and easy to miss. For my students, incremental improvements in their reasoning and writing are equally subtle.

We do have a choice. We can look at the tasks of our lives and separate them into work or gym. Just as we might choose to use the stairs instead of the elevator, or walk instead of calling an Uber, we can wall off our cognitive gym tasks from AI and ensure that we don’t lose our skills to this technology. And we can do the same when we assign a job to someone else. If it’s a work task, we can have AI do it. If it’s a gym task, it’s a waste of everyone’s time to give it to an AI because no one learns or gets stronger as a result.

Similarly, a future where AI generates words and images is one where society has to make choices about how it will treat its creatives. This won’t be the first time – today there is minimal demand for portrait painters, for example – but maybe this time we can make different, more deliberate, choices about the value of art in our society.

AI is going to fundamentally change the nature of work. Not nearly as fast as the AI companies want you to believe, but eventually it will. Policy analysis will definitely involve AI from now on, and my students need to reimagine what it means to learn and practice that skill. More generally, the line between work and gym will change in the future as we humans adapt ourselves to a world with these new intelligences.

But for now, the work v gym distinction is pretty clear. Use it on yourself.

  • Bruce Schneier is a security technologist who teaches at the Harvard Kennedy School at Harvard University and University of Toronto’s Munk School

blocks.html_en: I teach public policy at the Harvard Kennedy School and the Munk School at the University of Toronto. And it will come as no surprise to you that my students regularly use AI to complete their writing assignments. Doing so is a waste of their tuition money. But if their entire career is going to include AI writing assistants, why shouldn’t they embrace their future?

sentences.en: I teach public policy at the Harvard Kennedy School and the Munk School at the University of Toronto.
sentences.zh: 我在哈佛大学肯尼迪学院和多伦多大学蒙克学院教授公共政策。

sentences.en: And it will come as no surprise to you that my students regularly use AI to complete their writing assignments.
sentences.zh: 我的学生经常使用人工智能来完成他们的写作作业，这对你来说并不奇怪。

sentences.en: Doing so is a waste of their tuition money.
sentences.zh: 这样做就是浪费他们的学费。

sentences.en: But if their entire career is going to include AI writing assistants, why shouldn’t they embrace their future?
sentences.zh: 但如果他们的整个职业生涯都将包括人工智能写作助理，为什么他们不应该拥抱自己的未来呢？

blocks.html_en: The best way I’ve found to explain the dilemma comes from the AI researcher Daniel Meissler: it’s the difference between work and the gym.

sentences.en: The best way I’ve found to explain the dilemma comes from the AI researcher Daniel Meissler: it’s the difference between work and the gym.
sentences.zh: 我发现解释这一困境的最好方法来自人工智能研究员 Daniel Meissler：这是工作和健身房之间的区别。

blocks.html_en: At work, if your job is to move a bunch of heavy things from one side of the room to another, you should use whatever assistive tech you have on hand: a wagon, a forklift ... even an AI-powered robot. But at the gym, it makes no sense for that robot to lift weights for you. The point of weightlifting isn’t to move heavy things across the room; it’s to actually lift those heavy things.

sentences.en: At work, if your job is to move a bunch of heavy things from one side of the room to another, you should use whatever assistive tech you have on hand: a wagon, a forklift ...
sentences.zh: 在工作中，如果您的工作是将一堆重物从房间的一侧移动到另一侧，您应该使用手头的任何辅助技术：货车、叉车......

sentences.en: even an AI-powered robot.
sentences.zh: 甚至是人工智能驱动的机器人。

sentences.en: But at the gym, it makes no sense for that robot to lift weights for you.
sentences.zh: 但在健身房里，机器人为你举重是没有意义的。

sentences.en: The point of weightlifting isn’t to move heavy things across the room; it’s to actually lift those heavy things.
sentences.zh: 举重的目的不是将重物移过房间；而是将重物移过房间。它实际上是举起那些重的东西。

blocks.html_en: The same analysis holds for any task an AI can do for you. If it’s work – if the task has to be done and no one cares how – then it’s fine to use AI assistance. But if the task is more like the gym, and how the task is done is at least as important, then it probably doesn’t make sense to use AI.

sentences.en: The same analysis holds for any task an AI can do for you.
sentences.zh: 同样的分析适用于人工智能可以为你完成的任何任务。

sentences.en: If it’s work – if the task has to be done and no one cares how – then it’s fine to use AI assistance.
sentences.zh: 如果这是工作——如果任务必须完成而没有人关心如何完成——那么使用人工智能辅助就可以了。

sentences.en: But if the task is more like the gym, and how the task is done is at least as important, then it probably doesn’t make sense to use AI.
sentences.zh: 但如果任务更像是在健身房，并且任务的完成方式至少同样重要，那么使用人工智能可能就没有意义。

blocks.html_en: This, of course, assumes that the AI is actually up for the task and that it’s trustworthy: that it can do the job well, that its mistakes are minimal and correctable, that it’s been secured from cyber-attacks that would influence its results. Those are all important, and shouldn’t be minimized. There’s no point giving an AI something that it can’t do reliably. But once you’re confident that the AI can perform the task, the work v gym distinction helps you decide if it should.

sentences.en: This, of course, assumes that the AI is actually up for the task and that it’s trustworthy: that it can do the job well, that its mistakes are minimal and correctable, that it’s been secured from cyber-attacks that would influence its results.
sentences.zh: 当然，这是假设人工智能实际上能够胜任这项任务并且是值得信赖的：它可以很好地完成这项工作，它的错误很少并且可以纠正，它不会受到影响其结果的网络攻击。

sentences.en: Those are all important, and shouldn’t be minimized.
sentences.zh: 这些都很重要，不应该被忽视。

sentences.en: There’s no point giving an AI something that it can’t do reliably.
sentences.zh: 给人工智能做一些它无法可靠完成的事情是没有意义的。

sentences.en: But once you’re confident that the AI can perform the task, the work v gym distinction helps you decide if it should.
sentences.zh: 但是，一旦您确信人工智能可以执行该任务，工作与健身房的区别可以帮助您决定是否应该执行该任务。

I assign them because the very act of writing, which includes thinking and outlining and drafting and editing, making and criticizing and revising arguments, will help develop the critical thinking skills they will need in their future careers. And without this constant mental exercise, those skills will atrophy. Employers are already noticing.

sentences.zh: 我给学生布置的写作作业是体育任务，而不是工作任务。

sentences.en: I ask them to write policy memos not because the world needs more policy memos.
sentences.zh: 我要求他们写政策备忘录并不是因为世界需要更多政策备忘录。

sentences.en: I assign them because the very act of writing, which includes thinking and outlining and drafting and editing, making and criticizing and revising arguments, will help develop the critical thinking skills they will need in their future careers.
sentences.zh: 我分配给他们是因为写作的行为，包括思考、提纲、起草、编辑、提出、批评和修改论点，将有助于培养他们在未来职业生涯中所需的批判性思维技能。

sentences.en: And without this constant mental exercise, those skills will atrophy.
sentences.zh: 如果没有这种持续的脑力锻炼，这些技能就会萎缩。

sentences.en: Employers are already noticing.
sentences.zh: 雇主们已经注意到了。

sentences.zh: 至少今天，我可以很容易地区分人工智能编写的备忘录和学生编写的备忘录之间的区别——特别是如果学生只是交出聊天机器人生成的内容。

sentences.en: It’s a catchy, plausible, grammatically perfect essay that’s not particularly well-crafted or logically coherent – and with all the tells of mid-2026 AI-generated writing.
sentences.zh: 这是一篇引人入胜、看似合理、语法完美的文章，但不是特别精心制作或逻辑连贯——并且讲述了 2026 年中期人工智能生成的写作。

sentences.en: But it’s precisely because I have spent years developing my own writing skills that I’m able to identify prose that sounds great but doesn’t actually make sense.
sentences.zh: 但正是因为我花了多年的时间发展自己的写作技巧，我才能够识别出听起来很棒但实际上没有意义的散文。

sentences.en: My students don’t have that skill; they mistakenly view a confident, well-written essay as evidence of the quality of their ideas.
sentences.zh: 我的学生没有这项技能；他们错误地将一篇自信的、写得好的论文视为他们想法质量的证据。

sentences.en: They see the AI as cleaning those ideas up, getting them through that uncomfortable stretch of having to turn those ideas into prose.
sentences.zh: 他们认为人工智能正在清理这些想法，帮助他们度过将这些想法变成散文的不舒服的时期。

sentences.en: What the students miss is that their initial discomfort is a normal and healthy stage of writing, and not something to quickly get beyond.
sentences.zh: 学生们怀念的是，他们最初的不适是写作的一个正常而健康的阶段，而不是很快就能克服的阶段。

sentences.en: The very act of struggling with how to express what they think is an important part of the process.
sentences.zh: 为如何表达自己的想法而苦苦挣扎的行为本身就是这个过程的重要组成部分。

sentences.en: It’s how they test out their ideas, examine their hypotheses, and actually figure out what they think.
sentences.zh: 这是他们检验自己的想法、检验自己的假设并真正弄清楚自己的想法的方式。

sentences.en: Homework is not work; it’s the gym.
sentences.zh: 家庭作业不是工作；这是健身房。

blocks.html_en: Work v gym also helps us understand the problem facing creatives of all kinds.

sentences.en: Work v gym also helps us understand the problem facing creatives of all kinds.
sentences.zh: 工作与健身房还可以帮助我们了解各种创意人员面临的问题。

sentences.zh: 大多数时候，当有人雇用作家时，他们只需要文字。

sentences.en: They need an instruction manual for a piece of equipment, a detailed sales presentation, a government-mandated disclosure document, or a legal brief.

sentences.en: They need dry, predictable, accurate writing: a piece of work, exactly what AIs are good at today and what I don’t want in my student assignments.
sentences.zh: 他们需要干巴巴、可预测、准确的写作：一件作品，正是人工智能今天所擅长的，以及我在学生作业中不想要的。

sentences.en: Only sometimes is writing an art form – a book, a poem, an uplifting political speech.
sentences.zh: 只是有时写作才是一种艺术形式——一本书、一首诗、一篇振奋人心的政治演讲。

sentences.en: That kind of writing is more like the gym: process matters just as much as product.
sentences.zh: 这种写作更像是健身房：过程和产品一样重要。

sentences.zh: 在人类历史的大部分时间里，完成所有这些任务的唯一选择是人类作家。

sentences.en: We hired one regardless of whether we needed work writing or gym writing.
sentences.zh: 无论我们需要工作写作还是体育写作，我们都聘请了一位。

sentences.en: And that paid a lot of writers’ salaries.
sentences.zh: 这支付了很多作家的薪水。

sentences.en: I know fiction writers who supported that poorly paying career with lucrative technical writing work.
sentences.zh: 我认识一些小说作家，他们通过利润丰厚的技术写作工作来维持微薄的职业生涯。

sentences.en: Now, for the first time in human history, we can separate out when we need writing as work and when we want writing as gym.
sentences.zh: 现在，人类历史上第一次，我们可以区分何时需要将写作作为工作，何时需要将写作作为健身。

sentences.en: And if AI can do most of the work-type writing, society doesn’t need as many human writers.
sentences.zh: 如果人工智能可以完成大部分工作类型的写作，那么社会就不需要那么多的人类作家。

Historically we gave those jobs to artists, and sometimes beautiful art resulted. But most of the time it was just work. And, as it turns out, the world needs less pure art than simple images.

sentences.en: It’s the same for visual artists.
sentences.zh: 对于视觉艺术家来说也是如此。

sentences.en: Sometimes we need an actual artist, but most of the time we just need an image: a corporate mascot, a “beware of the dog” sign, or a packaging label.
sentences.zh: 有时我们需要一位真正的艺术家，但大多数时候我们只需要一个图像：企业吉祥物、“当心狗”标志或包装标签。

sentences.en: Historically we gave those jobs to artists, and sometimes beautiful art resulted.
sentences.zh: 从历史上看，我们把这些工作交给了艺术家，有时会产生美丽的艺术作品。

sentences.en: But most of the time it was just work.
sentences.zh: 但大多数时候这只是工作。

sentences.en: And, as it turns out, the world needs less pure art than simple images.
sentences.zh: 事实证明，世界需要的不是纯粹的艺术，而是简单的图像。

blocks.html_en: Explaining the problem isn’t the same as providing the solution. I give my students the “work versus gym” speech every class, but they still use AI. I have sympathy: assignments are hard, everyone is overworked and overstressed, and – most importantly – students feel like they’ll look bad in comparison if their peers are all using AI. Even if they don’t want to use the technology, they feel like they have no choice.

sentences.en: Explaining the problem isn’t the same as providing the solution.
sentences.zh: 解释问题并不等于提供解决方案。

sentences.en: I give my students the “work versus gym” speech every class, but they still use AI.
sentences.zh: 我每节课都会给我的学生做“工作与健身”的演讲，但他们仍然使用人工智能。

sentences.en: I have sympathy: assignments are hard, everyone is overworked and overstressed, and – most importantly – students feel like they’ll look bad in comparison if their peers are all using AI.
sentences.zh: 我对此表示同情：作业很辛苦，每个人都劳累过度、压力过大，而且最重要的是，学生们觉得，如果他们的同龄人都在使用人工智能，那么相比之下，他们会显得很糟糕。

sentences.en: Even if they don’t want to use the technology, they feel like they have no choice.
sentences.zh: 即使他们不想使用这项技术，他们也觉得自己别无选择。

blocks.html_en: There’s also an incentive problem. No one pays us to go to the gym; maintaining healthy habits requires discipline. For me, the payoffs to exercise – fewer aches and pains, less fatigue, better mood/stress management – might make me a better writer and teacher, but they’re subtle and easy to miss. For my students, incremental improvements in their reasoning and writing are equally subtle.

sentences.en: There’s also an incentive problem.
sentences.zh: 还有一个激励问题。

sentences.en: No one pays us to go to the gym; maintaining healthy habits requires discipline.
sentences.zh: 没有人付钱让我们去健身房；保持健康的习惯需要纪律。

sentences.en: For me, the payoffs to exercise – fewer aches and pains, less fatigue, better mood/stress management – might make me a better writer and teacher, but they’re subtle and easy to miss.
sentences.zh: 对我来说，锻炼的回报——更少的疼痛、更少的疲劳、更好的情绪/压力管理——可能会让我成为更好的作家和老师，但它们很微妙，很容易被忽视。

sentences.en: For my students, incremental improvements in their reasoning and writing are equally subtle.
sentences.zh: 对于我的学生来说，他们的推理和写作的渐进式进步同样是微妙的。

blocks.html_en: We do have a choice. We can look at the tasks of our lives and separate them into work or gym. Just as we might choose to use the stairs instead of the elevator, or walk instead of calling an Uber, we can wall off our cognitive gym tasks from AI and ensure that we don’t lose our skills to this technology. And we can do the same when we assign a job to someone else. If it’s a work task, we can have AI do it. If it’s a gym task, it’s a waste of everyone’s time to give it to an AI because no one learns or gets stronger as a result.

sentences.en: We do have a choice.
sentences.zh: 我们确实有一个选择。

sentences.en: We can look at the tasks of our lives and separate them into work or gym.
sentences.zh: 我们可以审视生活中的任务，并将它们分为工作或健身。

sentences.en: Just as we might choose to use the stairs instead of the elevator, or walk instead of calling an Uber, we can wall off our cognitive gym tasks from AI and ensure that we don’t lose our skills to this technology.
sentences.zh: 就像我们可能选择使用楼梯而不是电梯，或者步行而不是叫 Uber 一样，我们可以将我们的认知健身房任务与人工智能隔离开来，并确保我们不会因为这项技术而失去我们的技能。

sentences.en: And we can do the same when we assign a job to someone else.
sentences.zh: 当我们将工作分配给别人时，我们也可以这样做。

sentences.en: If it’s a work task, we can have AI do it.
sentences.zh: 如果这是一项工作任务，我们可以让人工智能来完成。

sentences.en: If it’s a gym task, it’s a waste of everyone’s time to give it to an AI because no one learns or gets stronger as a result.
sentences.zh: 如果这是一项健身房任务，那么把它交给人工智能就是浪费每个人的时间，因为没有人能够学习或变得更强壮。

blocks.html_en: Similarly, a future where AI generates words and images is one where society has to make choices about how it will treat its creatives. This won’t be the first time – today there is minimal demand for portrait painters, for example – but maybe this time we can make different, more deliberate, choices about the value of art in our society.

sentences.en: Similarly, a future where AI generates words and images is one where society has to make choices about how it will treat its creatives.
sentences.zh: 同样，在人工智能生成文字和图像的未来，社会必须选择如何对待其创意人员。

sentences.en: This won’t be the first time – today there is minimal demand for portrait painters, for example – but maybe this time we can make different, more deliberate, choices about the value of art in our society.
sentences.zh: 这不是第一次——例如，今天对肖像画家的需求很少——但也许这一次我们可以对艺术在我们社会中的价值做出不同的、更深思熟虑的选择。

blocks.html_en: AI is going to fundamentally change the nature of work. Not nearly as fast as the AI companies want you to believe, but eventually it will. Policy analysis will definitely involve AI from now on, and my students need to reimagine what it means to learn and practice that skill. More generally, the line between work and gym will change in the future as we humans adapt ourselves to a world with these new intelligences.

sentences.en: AI is going to fundamentally change the nature of work.
sentences.zh: 人工智能将从根本上改变工作的性质。

sentences.en: Not nearly as fast as the AI companies want you to believe, but eventually it will.
sentences.zh: 虽然不像人工智能公司希望你相信的那么快，但最终会的。

sentences.en: Policy analysis will definitely involve AI from now on, and my students need to reimagine what it means to learn and practice that skill.
sentences.zh: 从现在开始，政策分析肯定会涉及人工智能，我的学生需要重新想象学习和练习这项技能意味着什么。

sentences.en: More generally, the line between work and gym will change in the future as we humans adapt ourselves to a world with these new intelligences.
sentences.zh: 更一般地说，随着我们人类适应一个拥有这些新智能的世界，工作和健身之间的界限在未来将会改变。

blocks.html_en: But for now, the work v gym distinction is pretty clear. Use it on yourself.

sentences.en: But for now, the work v gym distinction is pretty clear.
sentences.zh: 但就目前而言，工作与健身房的区别非常明显。

sentences.en: Use it on yourself.
sentences.zh: 用在自己身上。

blocks.html_en: • Bruce Schneier is a security technologist who teaches at the Harvard Kennedy School at Harvard University and University of Toronto’s Munk School

sentences.en: Bruce Schneier is a security technologist who teaches at the Harvard Kennedy School at Harvard University and University of Toronto’s Munk School
sentences.zh: Bruce Schneier 是一名安全技术专家，在哈佛大学肯尼迪学院和多伦多大学蒙克学院任教

blocks.html_en: Bruce Schneier is a security technologist who teaches at the Harvard Kennedy School at Harvard University and University of Toronto’s Munk School

sentences.en: Bruce Schneier is a security technologist who teaches at the Harvard Kennedy School at Harvard University and University of Toronto’s Munk School
sentences.zh: Bruce Schneier 是一名安全技术专家，在哈佛大学肯尼迪学院和多伦多大学蒙克学院任教

content_html_zh:
我在哈佛大学肯尼迪学院和多伦多大学蒙克学院教授公共政策。 我的学生经常使用人工智能来完成他们的写作作业，这对你来说并不奇怪。 这样做就是浪费他们的学费。 但如果他们的整个职业生涯都将包括人工智能写作助理，为什么他们不应该拥抱自己的未来呢？

我发现解释这一困境的最好方法来自人工智能研究员 Daniel Meissler：这是工作和健身房之间的区别。

在工作中，如果您的工作是将一堆重物从房间的一侧移动到另一侧，您应该使用手头的任何辅助技术：货车、叉车...... 甚至是人工智能驱动的机器人。 但在健身房里，机器人为你举重是没有意义的。 举重的目的不是将重物移过房间；而是将重物移过房间。它实际上是举起那些重的东西。

同样的分析适用于人工智能可以为你完成的任何任务。 如果这是工作——如果任务必须完成而没有人关心如何完成——那么使用人工智能辅助就可以了。 但如果任务更像是在健身房，并且任务的完成方式至少同样重要，那么使用人工智能可能就没有意义。

当然，这是假设人工智能实际上能够胜任这项任务并且是值得信赖的：它可以很好地完成这项工作，它的错误很少并且可以纠正，它不会受到影响其结果的网络攻击。 这些都很重要，不应该被忽视。 给人工智能做一些它无法可靠完成的事情是没有意义的。 但是，一旦您确信人工智能可以执行该任务，工作与健身房的区别可以帮助您决定是否应该执行该任务。

我给学生布置的写作作业是体育任务，而不是工作任务。 我要求他们写政策备忘录并不是因为世界需要更多政策备忘录。 我分配给他们是因为写作的行为，包括思考、提纲、起草、编辑、提出、批评和修改论点，将有助于培养他们在未来职业生涯中所需的批判性思维技能。 如果没有这种持续的脑力锻炼，这些技能就会萎缩。 雇主们已经注意到了。

阅读他们提交的作业，我可以看到我的学生的这些技能要么蓬勃发展，要么萎缩。 至少今天，我可以很容易地区分人工智能编写的备忘录和学生编写的备忘录之间的区别——特别是如果学生只是交出聊天机器人生成的内容。 这是一篇引人入胜、看似合理、语法完美的文章，但不是特别精心制作或逻辑连贯——并且讲述了 2026 年中期人工智能生成的写作。

但正是因为我花了多年的时间发展自己的写作技巧，我才能够识别出听起来很棒但实际上没有意义的散文。 我的学生没有这项技能；他们错误地将一篇自信的、写得好的论文视为他们想法质量的证据。 他们认为人工智能正在清理这些想法，帮助他们度过将这些想法变成散文的不舒服的时期。 学生们怀念的是，他们最初的不适是写作的一个正常而健康的阶段，而不是很快就能克服的阶段。 为如何表达自己的想法而苦苦挣扎的行为本身就是这个过程的重要组成部分。 这是他们检验自己的想法、检验自己的假设并真正弄清楚自己的想法的方式。 家庭作业不是工作；这是健身房。

工作与健身房还可以帮助我们了解各种创意人员面临的问题。

大多数时候，当有人雇用作家时，他们只需要文字。 他们需要设备的使用说明书、详细的销售演示、政府规定的披露文件或法律摘要。 他们需要干巴巴、可预测、准确的写作：一件作品，正是人工智能今天所擅长的，以及我在学生作业中不想要的。 只是有时写作才是一种艺术形式——一本书、一首诗、一篇振奋人心的政治演讲。 这种写作更像是健身房：过程和产品一样重要。

在人类历史的大部分时间里，完成所有这些任务的唯一选择是人类作家。 无论我们需要工作写作还是体育写作，我们都聘请了一位。 这支付了很多作家的薪水。 我认识一些小说作家，他们通过利润丰厚的技术写作工作来维持微薄的职业生涯。 现在，人类历史上第一次，我们可以区分何时需要将写作作为工作，何时需要将写作作为健身。 如果人工智能可以完成大部分工作类型的写作，那么社会就不需要那么多的人类作家。

对于视觉艺术家来说也是如此。 有时我们需要一位真正的艺术家，但大多数时候我们只需要一个图像：企业吉祥物、“当心狗”标志或包装标签。 从历史上看，我们把这些工作交给了艺术家，有时会产生美丽的艺术作品。 但大多数时候这只是工作。 事实证明，世界需要的不是纯粹的艺术，而是简单的图像。

解释问题并不等于提供解决方案。 我每节课都会给我的学生做“工作与健身”的演讲，但他们仍然使用人工智能。 我对此表示同情：作业很辛苦，每个人都劳累过度、压力过大，而且最重要的是，学生们觉得，如果他们的同龄人都在使用人工智能，那么相比之下，他们会显得很糟糕。 即使他们不想使用这项技术，他们也觉得自己别无选择。

还有一个激励问题。 没有人付钱让我们去健身房；保持健康的习惯需要纪律。 对我来说，锻炼的回报——更少的疼痛、更少的疲劳、更好的情绪/压力管理——可能会让我成为更好的作家和老师，但它们很微妙，很容易被忽视。 对于我的学生来说，他们的推理和写作的渐进式进步同样是微妙的。

我们确实有一个选择。 我们可以审视生活中的任务，并将它们分为工作或健身。 就像我们可能选择使用楼梯而不是电梯，或者步行而不是叫 Uber 一样，我们可以将我们的认知健身房任务与人工智能隔离开来，并确保我们不会因为这项技术而失去我们的技能。 当我们将工作分配给别人时，我们也可以这样做。 如果这是一项工作任务，我们可以让人工智能来完成。 如果这是一项健身房任务，那么把它交给人工智能就是浪费每个人的时间，因为没有人能够学习或变得更强壮。

同样，在人工智能生成文字和图像的未来，社会必须选择如何对待其创意人员。 这不是第一次——例如，今天对肖像画家的需求很少——但也许这一次我们可以对艺术在我们社会中的价值做出不同的、更深思熟虑的选择。

人工智能将从根本上改变工作的性质。 虽然不像人工智能公司希望你相信的那么快，但最终会的。 从现在开始，政策分析肯定会涉及人工智能，我的学生需要重新想象学习和练习这项技能意味着什么。 更一般地说，随着我们人类适应一个拥有这些新智能的世界，工作和健身之间的界限在未来将会改变。

但就目前而言，工作与健身房的区别非常明显。 用在自己身上。

  • Bruce Schneier 是一名安全技术专家，在哈佛大学肯尼迪学院和多伦多大学蒙克学院任教
